qa_pairs.question: What is Galaxy and what is its primary purpose in research?
qa_pairs.answer: Galaxy is an open-source, web-based platform designed for data-intensive biomedical research. Its main purpose is to make computational biology accessible to research scientists who do not possess computer programming or systems administration experience.

qa_pairs.question: How do Canadian users access UseGalaxy Canada, and what is the initial storage allocation provided?
qa_pairs.answer: Canadian users can access UseGalaxy Canada by visiting [https://starthere.usegalaxy.ca](https://starthere.usegalaxy.ca), following the link to log in through CILogon, and selecting their institution to authenticate. The platform then allocates a default starting storage of 500 GB per user.

qa_pairs.question: What are some key characteristics of the UseGalaxy Canada platform regarding user requirements and system management?
qa_pairs.answer: UseGalaxy Canada requires no Linux knowledge from its users. The Galaxy configuration and server configuration are entirely handled by the UseGalaxy Canada admins and team, respectively. The platform's servers run on Beluga Cloud & Arbutus, and pre-installed tools are synchronized with UseGalaxy.eu.
